title: PHP Arrays
content:
### PHP Arrays

An array is a data structure that stores multiple values in a single variable.

### Array Types

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| Indexed | Numeric keys (0, 1, 2...) | `["a", "b", "c"]` |
| Associative | Named keys | `["name" => "John"]` |
| Multidimensional | Arrays within arrays | `[[1,2], [3,4]]` |

### Creating Arrays

```php
<?php
// Using array() function (older syntax)
$colors = array("Red", "Green", "Blue");

// Using short syntax (PHP 5.4+)
$colors = ["Red", "Green", "Blue"];

// Mixed types
$mixed = [1, "hello", true, 3.14];

// Appending
$colors[] = "Yellow"; // Adds at the end
?>
```

### Array Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `count()` | Number of elements |
| `array_push()` | Add to end |
| `array_pop()` | Remove from end |
| `array_merge()` | Merge arrays |
| `in_array()` | Check if value exists |
| `sort()` | Sort array |

> **Key Point:** Arrays in PHP are actually ordered maps. A map associates values to keys. This type is optimized for several uses.
codeExample:
<?php
// Create arrays
$fruits = ["Apple", "Banana", "Orange"];
$person = [
    "name" => "John",
    "age" => 30,
    "city" => "New York"
];

// Access elements
echo $fruits[0]; // Apple
echo $person["name"]; // John

// Check length
echo count($fruits); // 3

// Add element
$fruits[] = "Mango";
print_r($fruits);
?>

Which function returns the number of elements in an array?
